a:T36d63,<div class="specification">
<p><span class="fontstyle0">The kinetics of an enzyme-catalysed reaction are studied in the absence and presence of an inhibitor. The graph represents the initial rate as a function of substrate concentration.</span></p>
<p><br/> <img height="472" src="data:image/png;base64," width="659"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p><span class="fontstyle0">Identify the type of inhibition shown in the graph.</span></p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p><span class="fontstyle0">Determine the value of </span><math class="wrs_chemistry" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>V</mi><mi>max</mi></msub></math> <span class="fontstyle0">and </span><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></msub></math> <span class="fontstyle0">in the absence and presence of the inhibitor.</span></p>
<p><span class="fontstyle0"><img height="218" src="data:image/png;base64," width="536"/></span></p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b(i).</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p><span class="fontstyle0">Outline the significance of the value of the Michaelis constant, </span><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></msub></math><span class="fontstyle0">.</span></p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b(ii).</div>
</div>b:T27ac,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>non-competitive «inhibition» ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/><br/>✔✔✔</p>
<p><em>Award <strong>[3]</strong> for four values correct.</em><br/><em>Award <strong>[2]</strong> for three values correct.</em><br/><em>Award <strong>[1]</strong> for two values correct.</em><br/><em>Ignore units.</em><br/><em>Accept <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo mathvariant="italic">±</mo><mn mathvariant="italic">0</mn><mo mathvariant="italic">.</mo><mn mathvariant="italic">1</mn></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">m</mi></msub></math> and <math class="wrs_chemistry" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>V</mi><mrow><mi>m</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msub></math>. </em><em>No ECF applied.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b(i).</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></msub></math> is an inverse measure of affinity of <span style="text-decoration: underline;">substrate</span> for enzyme<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>higher <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></msub></math> indicates higher <span style="text-decoration: underline;">substrate</span> concentration is needed for enzyme saturation<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>low value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></msub></math> means reaction is fast at low <span style="text-decoration: underline;">substrate</span> concentration ✔</p>
<p><br/><em>Idea of “inverse relationship” must be conveyed.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b(ii).</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/chemistry/topic/b/","children":["← back to ","Chemistry"," topic ","b"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"20N.3.HL.TZ0.10"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Chemistry"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"20N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","b-7-proteins-and-enzymes"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Most scored the one mark here for identifying the correct type of inhibition, namely, non-competitive inhibition.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>The stronger candidates scored all three marks here for the four correct values. Most scored at least one mark for the V<sub>max</sub> values in the absence and presence of the inhibitor. The Km values sometimes were outside the permitted ±0.1 range.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b(i).</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>The stronger candidates scored all three marks here for the four correct values. Most scored at least one mark for the V<sub>max</sub> values in the absence and presence of the inhibitor. The Km values sometimes were outside the permitted ±0.1 range.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b(ii).</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
